The Welcome Letter

Welcome to the World

You are holding a piece of a vision, a chapter in a theme, and an invitation into a world where every version of you is honored —

the bold and the tender, the grounded and the wild.

We design through themes, not seasons.

We exist to create pieces that honor your complexity —

your softness and your strength,
your stillness and your fire.

Sincerely,

01 — The Brand

The Truth of

For:Marketing AgencyPR AgencyAV EngineerWebsite Developer

is not a fashion brand that tells stories. It is a story house — born in Cairo — that expresses itself through fashion, objects, and real-world experiences. Every collection is a world. Every world is a chapter. Every chapter is a lived philosophy.

We design through themes, not seasons. We exist to create pieces that honor complexity — your softness and your strength, your stillness and your fire. The brand is bold, wild, colorful, and unapologetically layered — a pop-luxury house with a philosophical core.

The Brand Statement

“Born to celebrate all layers of you.” — This is not a tagline. It is a position. A declaration that makes to every person who enters its world: you are not one thing, and we will never ask you to be.

Brand Characteristics

SophisticatedWildly ConfidentInnovativeStory-DrivenBoldDominantLuxuriousMemorableChicAspiring

The Target Client

DimensionProfile
Age RangeAffluent adults, 20–65
IncomeHigh & medium-high earners
LocationGlobal — urban cities, gated communities, cosmopolitan centres
IdentityThe Global Citizen — well-traveled, culturally fluent, cosmopolitan
LifestyleTravel, fashion, entertainment & leisure, highly maintained shopping habits
MindsetTrendsetter, established professional, collector of meaning
02 — Architecture

Three Worlds, One Soul

For:Marketing AgencyPR AgencyWebsite Developer

operates across three distinct but interconnected worlds. Each world has its own audience entry point, price positioning, and emotional register — yet all three trace back to the same philosophical core.

The Luxury Line

House

The pinnacle. Every piece is a direct translation of the Chapter narrative — designed with the precision of a couturier and the imagination of an artist. The fan lives here. Numbered editions live here.

True contemporary luxury pricing

The Lifestyle Line

Life

The everyday luxury expression. Wearable, deeply in spirit — built for the life lived between extraordinary moments. The entry into the world for the client who will ascend to the House.

Premium accessible luxury pricing

Bespoke Services

Atelier

Not customization — collaborative authorship. A one-to-one creative conversation where the client’s own story becomes the brief. By appointment. By conversation. By story.

Quoted · Private · Significant

The Chapter System

does not follow a fashion calendar. It releases Chapters — each one a complete thematic world with its own name, narrative, visual identity, hero object, and experiential counterpart. Each Chapter contains: The Story · The House Collection · The Life Collection · The Hero Object · The Experience · The Archive Entry.

CategoryLineLaunch Phase

FragranceLife + AtelierChapter Two

03 — Chapter One

Hannah The Dancer

For:Marketing AgencyPR AgencyAV EngineerWebsite Developer

“Ignorance is not absence — it is beauty that conceals. Light that blinds. Motion that traps. Hannah dances at the centre of her own world, unaware that the universe extends far beyond her wings.”

Hannah is the first of seven chapters in the philosophical universe. She represents Stage One: Ignorance — the first stage on the journey toward ultimate wisdom. Hannah The Dancer is euphoric, excessive, electric. Ignorance here is bliss made beautiful.

The Hannah Creative World

ElementMeaning
The DragonflyThe human spirit in transformation. All-seeing yet choosing blindness in the party of her own making.
The StarsEnergy, movement, celebration. The electric dynamic vibe of The Party. Northern Star is a core brand element.
Fireworks & Shattered GlassThe chaos of noise and distraction. The beautiful destruction of ignorance at its peak.
The PartyStage One — not a moral failure. A necessary beginning. The dance before the awakening.
The FanIgnorance made physical. Cairo-designed and manufactured. Chapter One hero object. Numbered edition.

The Hannah Folding Fan — Hero Object Brief

The first folding fan designed and manufactured in Egypt. Individually numbered — first edition, Chapter One only. When Hannah closes, the fan retires forever. The object tells the story in gesture alone — opened, it reveals Hannah’s world. Closed, the world disappears.

Hannah — Bespoke Party Experience

Under Atelier: The Hannah Party Experience. designs the wardrobe, the objects, the atmosphere, the narrative — a complete thematic world authored for one person or group. Not event planning. Invitation-only at launch.

04 — Visual Identity

Brand CI & Visual System

For:Marketing AgencyPR AgencyAV EngineerWebsite Developer

The Color Palette

Princeton Orange

HEX · #FF6808
RGB · 255 / 104 / 8
CMYK · 0 / 73 / 100 / 0

Champagne

HEX · #FCE9D2
RGB · 252 / 233 / 210
CMYK · 1 / 8 / 17 / 0

Cerise Pink

HEX · #DB2E6D
RGB · 219 / 46 / 109
CMYK · 9 / 96 / 34 / 0

Lavender Pink

HEX · #FEC9FC
RGB · 254 / 198 / 252
CMYK · 4 / 25 / 0 / 0

Princeton Orange is the signature — primary color of the house, used at full strength. Cerise Pink marks moments of highest impact. Lavender Pink is the brand’s romantic accent — used for tenderness, softness, and feminine moments. Champagne is the brand’s voice of sophistication — used as typography on color grounds and as a refined neutral. No other colors enter the world.

Color Discipline

The palette is non-negotiable. Princeton Orange, Champagne, Cerise Pink, Lavender Pink only. No supplementary colors, no greys, no blacks. The four colors operate as a complete ecosystem with defined roles.

Color Application Guidelines

ApplicationPrimaryAccentType Color
Packaging — OuterPrinceton OrangeCerise + Lavender PinkChampagne
Packaging — Inner / TissueChampagnePrinceton OrangeCerise Pink
Welcome CardPrinceton Orange fieldCerise + Lavender accentsChampagne
Website Hero SectionsPrinceton OrangeCerise + Lavender accentsChampagne
Social Media — Hero PostsPrinceton OrangeCerise PinkChampagne
Social Media — Romantic / Soft PostsLavender PinkChampagneCerise Pink
All Press MaterialsPrinceton OrangeCeriseChampagne
Atelier / Bespoke MaterialsLavender Pink dominantCerise + Princeton OrangeCerise Pink on Lavender / Champagne on Cerise

Logo Usage Rules — Don’ts

  • Do not distort the logo — the wordmark and emblem proportions are fixed. Never compress, skew, or warp.
  • Do not rotate or stretch the logo — the emblem and wordmark always sit upright at their original aspect ratio.
  • Do not use different colors — logo and emblem appear only in the four official brand colors. Never green, never black, never any non-palette tone.
  • Do not use the trademark with the logo as a logo — the wordmark and the emblem are used independently. Never lock them together as a combined mark.

Brand Mark System

ElementDescriptionUsage Rule
Wordmark — Rounded geometric sans-serif. Clean, confident, architectural. The hyphen is non-negotiable — always present.Always in Champagne on Orange or Cerise grounds. Never drop the hyphen. Used independently from the emblem.
Northern Star EmblemEight-point star with cerise inner compass and champagne rays. The celestial symbol.Primary emblem. Appears on the fan near the handle. Used as chapter divider and background watermark. Never combined with wordmark as a single locked logo.
Script Signature“By Sophia G.” — handwritten, personal, authored.Reserved for objects and bespoke pieces only. Never in mass communications.

Typography System

RoleTypefaceUsage
Display / Headlines (Serif)Cormorant Garamond — editorial, timeless elegance, italic weightCampaign headlines, Chapter names, pull quotes, hero copy, card titles, section titles
Brand / UI / Body (Sans-Serif)Jost — geometric, clean, modern, multiple weightsNavigation, labels, tags, product naming, body copy, UI elements, data tables
05 — AV Show Brief

Hannah — The AI Film

For:AV Engineer — PrimaryMarketing Agency

“This is not a campaign video. It is a visual mythology — an AI-generated audio-visual piece that tells the story of Ignorance from the inside. Made to be experienced on a large screen with sound that moves through the body.”

Project Overview

SpecificationDetail
Project TitleHannah The Dancer — An Audio-Visual Experience by
FormatAI-generated audio-visual film
Live Show Duration10–12 minutes (full experience)
Digital Cut90 seconds (social & online release)
Press Cut3 minutes (media & PR)
Output Format4K minimum / Large-format projection ready
Color SpacePrinceton Orange #FF6808 · Champagne #FCE9D2 · Cerise Pink #DB2E6D · Lavender Pink #FEC9FC. No black.
Sound DesignCollaborative — to be scored by international DJ partner
DeliveryPre-launch digital release (48hrs before event) + Live show

Narrative Arc — Three Acts

Opening Sequence
The Arrival

A field of champagne. A single point of cerise — the northern star emblem. It multiplies into a constellation. Becomes a sky of orange, cerise and lavender points. We fall into the world from above. The emblem dissolves into the scene. We are entering Hannah’s world without knowing it yet.

Act One — 0:00 to 4:00
The Party — Pure Euphoria

We are inside the party. Two dragonflies — Hannah and her counterpart — their wings refracting into Princeton Orange and Cerise Pink with lavender shimmer. Stars burst in every direction. Sound is all-consuming. Hannah is the protagonist of her world. Color at full weapon-strength.

Act Two — 4:00 to 9:00
The Blindness — What She Cannot See

The camera begins to notice what Hannah cannot. Champagne and lavender shadows at the edges of the frame. Stillness beneath the motion. Doors she moves past without seeing. The audience sees — Hannah dances on.

Act Three — 9:00 to 12:00
The Crack — and The Promise

One moment. A single beat of silence. Hannah pauses — almost. The fan opens fully in her hand. For one frame the world fades to champagne. Then the music returns. Final frame: the northern star emblem — cerise on lavender, single, still. Text: “This is Chapter One. There are six more.” Fade.

Sound & Music Direction

  • Opening: Near silence — one sustained tone. The breath before the storm.
  • Act One: Builds to full techno euphoria. The party at maximum volume. Layered, overwhelming, beautiful.
  • Act Two: Music continues but something shifts beneath it. A counter-melody suggesting depth.
  • Act Three: A single beat of silence — mandatory. The fan opens in that silence. Then the return. Then the final note. Then nothing.

The Live Show Format

The 10–12 minute film plays on large-format projection or LED wall at the Cairo launch event, with an international DJ performing live alongside it. This is not a VJ set — it is a scored live premiere. The collection is worn in the room. The fan is held. This is the most complete sensory launch experience the Egyptian luxury market has ever seen.

06 — Digital Presence

Website & Digital Architecture

For:Website Developer — PrimaryMarketing Agency

The website is not an e-commerce platform with a story section. It is a world with a shop inside it. The experience of arriving on the site should feel like receiving an invitation — not browsing a catalogue.

Site Architecture

Entry Point
The World

Full-screen landing on Princeton Orange. The wordmark centered in champagne. The northern star emblem animates subtly in the background. One line of copy. One entry point. No clutter.

Section 01
The Chapter — Hannah

The current Chapter world in full. The story published as a narrative page on orange ground with champagne text. The collection presented editorially, not as a product grid. The fan has its own dedicated page.

Section 02
The House — The Life — The Atelier

Three distinct world entries. House and Life on orange. Atelier on lavender pink with cerise typography — the romantic, intimate corner of the brand. The Hannah Party Experience described. Enquiry-only.

Section 03
The Archive + Enter Your World

The permanent library. Chapter One documented. Future chapters signaled. An email capture on cerise ground: “Enter the world of .”

Technical Requirements

RequirementSpecificationPriority
PlatformCustom build preferred. Shopify with heavy custom theme acceptable. No template aesthetics visible.Critical
Color DisciplinePrinceton Orange, Champagne, Cerise Pink, Lavender Pink only. All text on color grounds in Champagne (or Cerise on Lavender).Critical
PerformanceSub-3 second load on mobile. Imagery optimised. Lazy loading throughout.Critical
Mobile FirstPrimary audience is global, mobile-native. Every decision mobile-first.Critical
Video IntegrationHannah AV film 90-second cut embeddable on Chapter One page. Autoplay muted, controls available.Critical
E-CommerceHouse + Life purchasable. Fan with numbered edition display. Atelier: enquiry form only.Critical
AnimationThe northern star emblem animates subtly. Transitions feel like turning a page.High
TypographyCormorant Garamond for display. Jost for UI and body. Never default system fonts.High
LanguageEnglish primary. Arabic secondary — cultural sensitivity, not auto-translation.Medium

Copy Direction

  • Hero CTA — never “Shop Now”: “Enter Chapter One” / “Step Into the World” / “Claim Your Piece”
  • Fan page headline: “Folded into your hand is a world. Open it slowly.”
  • Atelier page: “Not customization. A conversation. Bring your story — we will design the world around it.”
  • Email capture: “Enter the world of ” — a single field. No ‘subscribe’. No ‘newsletter’.
  • Product descriptions: Always open with narrative before material, sizing, care.
07 — Launch Strategy

The Opening — Chapter One

For:Marketing AgencyPR AgencyWebsite Developer

“The world doesn’t meet a brand. It receives an invitation into a world. does not launch — it opens.”

Three-Act Launch Sequence

ActPhaseTimingAction
Act IThe WhisperDays 1–7 pre-launchSingle image drops. The fan — partially open. One dragonfly wing visible. No caption. Just the wordmark. Then: a star. A fabric detail. A shadow. Nothing explained. Everything felt.
Act IIThe Film48 hours pre-launch90-second AI film cut releases online simultaneously with press outreach. Website goes live in soft mode.
Act IIIThe OpeningLaunch DayEverything arrives at once. Website fully live. Hannah story published. Collection available. Fan available — numbered, limited. Atelier page live. Welcome letter as first campaign post.
EventThe PartyLaunch Night — CairoThe AV show. The DJ. The collection worn in the room. The fan held. Cairo becomes the location of something that has never happened before. Documented as a short film.

Content Generated From One Launch

  • The AI film — lives permanently as ’s first major content asset.
  • Campaign imagery — 6–8 hero images, 15+ detail shots, 3–4 video pieces.
  • Behind the AV film — the production process is content. Document everything.
  • The event film — a short cinematic document of launch night. Not a highlight reel. A piece.
  • The fan story — origin, manufacturing, significance. Egypt-first. Deeply pressworthy.
  • The DJ collaboration announcement — their audience meets . Cultural crossover.
  • Client and guest organic content — the fan photographed, held, opened. Uncontrollable. Powerful.
08 — Marketing & PR

Reach & Recognition

For:Marketing Agency — PrimaryPR Agency — Primary

The Story Editors Will Open

“A Cairo-born luxury house has launched its first chapter — a seven-stage philosophical journey toward wisdom — beginning with Ignorance. Their debut object is the first folding fan designed and manufactured in Egypt. Their launch is an AI audio-visual experience scored live by an international DJ. The brand is called . Chapter One is Hannah The Dancer. There are six more coming.”

Press Target List

TierTargetAngle
Tier 1 — Global FashionVogue Arabia, Vogue, System Magazine, Document Journal, AnOther Magazine, i-DStory-driven luxury. The Chapter system. Egypt as origin. The fan as first object.
Tier 1 — CultureNowness, 1843, Monocle, Wallpaper*The AV show. The philosophical architecture. Cairo as the birthplace of something new.
Tier 2 — Luxury DigitalBusiness of Fashion, Hypebeast, Ssense Journal, HighsnobietyContemporary luxury disruptor. Pop-bold. Story-first. Anti-calendar.
Tier 2 — Music & CultureResident Advisor, DJ Mag, MixmagThe DJ collaboration. Fashion meets techno culture.
Tier 3 — RegionalEgyptian Streets, Ahram Online arts desk, regional lifestyle titlesCairo-born. Egypt-manufactured. The fan as a category first locally.

Social Media Strategy

PhaseContentTone
Pre-Launch WhisperFragments only. Fan. Wing. Star. Shadow. No product. No explanation.Silent. Intriguing. One image at a time.
Launch DayCampaign imagery. Welcome letter as first caption. Full Hannah story. Fan available. Film live.Declarative. World-opening. The house speaks for the first time.
Weeks 2–4Making of the fan. Campaign BTS. Individual piece stories. Event documentation. DJ announcement. Chapter Two hint.Intimate. Behind-the-world. Keep the universe alive and deepening.

Brand Voice

Speaks Like This

  • Poetic but precise — every word earns its place
  • Bold declarations — never timid suggestions
  • Culturally intelligent — references, allusions, trust
  • Intimate and knowing — speaks to one person
  • Wildly intelligent — the voice of an artist who builds things
  • Cinematic — each caption could be a film title

Never Sounds Like This

  • Vague romanticism without meaning
  • Status-driven or boastful
  • Trendy or reactive to the news cycle
  • Generic luxury — “crafted with care”
  • Over-explained — never tells, always shows
  • Promotional — “sale”, “discount”, “limited time”

Metrics That Matter at Launch

  • Email list signups — the highest-value asset. Quality over volume.
  • Website session depth — are people reading the story? Time on site matters more than traffic.
  • Press pickups — quality of publication, not quantity of articles.
  • Fan sell-through rate — the numbered edition. What percentage sells and how fast.
  • DM quality — who is reaching out, from where, why.
  • DJ / collaborator interest — how many meaningful creative conversations the AV announcement generates.
09 — Deliverables

What Each Team Needs

For:All Teams

Marketing & PR Agency

  • Brand Stake File — this document
  • Campaign imagery — hi-res, print-ready and web-optimized. Available post-shoot.
  • Press Kit PDF — brand story, Chapter One narrative, fan origin story, campaign selects, lookbook
  • 90-second AI film cut — embargoed until 48hrs pre-launch
  • Social content calendar — pre-launch whisper sequence through week 4
  • Welcome letter copy — signed off, locked
  • Press release — drafted from the single press angle in Section 08
  • Media contact list — tiered as outlined in Section 08

AV Engineer

  • Brand Stake File — this document, Sections 03–05 as primary brief
  • Full narrative arc document — three-act storyboard with beat-by-beat timing
  • Visual reference board — Refik Anadol, James Jean, Wong Kar-wai, Noé, Fantasia
  • Hannah fan design file — accurate reference for Act Three product appearance
  • Color technical specs — #FF6808 · #FCE9D2 · #DB2E6D · #FEC9FC. HDR notes, large-format display specs.
  • Sound brief — shared with DJ partner simultaneously for co-authorship
  • Three delivery formats — 10–12min live / 3min press / 90sec social

Website Developer

  • Brand Stake File — this document, Sections 04 and 06 as primary brief
  • Full CI assets — logo files, emblem files, color specs, typography files (Cormorant Garamond + Jost)
  • Campaign imagery — web-optimized, all formats. Available post-shoot.
  • 90-second film file — for Chapter One page integration
  • All copy — headlines, product descriptions, story text, CTAs. Locked and signed off.
  • E-commerce product list — SKUs, pricing, variant specs for House and Life lines
  • Atelier enquiry form spec — fields, routing, response protocol
  • Launch date — site must be live and load-tested 48hrs before launch
